description: The Office of Congressman Kweisi Mfume (MD-07) is currently accepting applications for Summer 2022 in our Baltimore and DC offices. Interns will learn about the legislative process, constituent services, and many other functions of a congressional office. The term of this internship will be from June - Early August.
responsibilities: Answer constituent letters on various issues before the House
Assist in various administrative tasks as needed
Work on special projects in the district
qualifications: District and Maryland ties are preferred but not required
Must be available to work at least 30 hours per week
how_to_apply: Interested applicants should email a cover letter, resume, and short writing sample to Md07employment@mail.house.gov by noon on April 22, 2022. Please be sure to note in the subject line 'Summer Internship' and which office you would prefer to work.
equal_opportunity: All applicants will be considered without regard to race, color, national origin, religion, sex, gender identity, disability, or age.

office: House Committee on Natural Resources (Democratic Staff)
description: The Democratic Staff of the House Committee on Natural Resources is currently seeking a full-time intern for Summer 2022 (May - August). We seek candidates who are passionate about issues within the Natural Resources Committee's jurisdiction and eager to learn about the committee process. Our office currently operates on a hybrid basis - M, F remote and Tu, W, Th in-person. Depending on guidance from the CDC and Office of the Attending Physician, internship format may be modified to remote work at any time.
qualifications: Passionate about issues within the Natural Resources Committee's jurisdiction
Eager to learn about the committee process
how_to_apply: Submit application materials to Anna Martin at NRD.Internships@mail.house.gov with the subject line 'Spring Internship Application - First Name Last Name' by COB April 15, 2022. Please include a cover letter, resume, short writing sample, and dates of availability. Applicants will be considered on a rolling basis. No phone calls, emails, or drop-ins please.
equal_opportunity: Women, LGBTQ+, and minority candidates are encouraged to apply. All applicants will be considered without regard to race, color, national origin, religion, sex, gender identity, disability, or age.

description: Congressman Gregory W. Meeks (NY-5) is seeking an enthusiastic, highly motivated Legislative and Press/Digital Interns to work in-person in our Washington, D.C. office for Summer 2022. Interns in Washington, D.C. are fully integrated into the day-to-day operations of our office. They are entrusted with a wide range of responsibilities to assist our legislative and communications staff. As an intern for Congressman Meeks, the Chairman of the House Foreign Affairs Committee and a senior member of the House Financial Services Committee, you will have a unique opportunity to gain unparalleled exposure to the political processes and legislative work on Capitol Hill. Qualified candidates will demonstrate a strong work ethic, attention to detail, and strong writing and editing skills.
qualifications: Demonstrate a strong and positive work ethic
Have strong writing and editing skills
Willing to follow through on a wide variety of tasks
how_to_apply: Please submit your resume, cover letter, and 2-3-page writing sample and graphic sample (if applicable) to meeks.house.gov. Please submit your application no later than Friday, April 22nd at 11:59 PM EST.

description:
Congressman Conor Lamb (PA-17) has openings for full and part-time congressional interns for his Washington, D.C. office during the Summer of 2022.

Candidates should be motivated, flexible, and detail-oriented individuals with availability beginning in May, and a preference for those who can stay at least three months. Intern responsibilities include answering constituent phone calls, leading tours of the Capitol, sorting mail and faxes, and assisting staff with projects in diverse issue areas.

Candidates should be outgoing and possess strong oral and written communication skills, an ability to multitask in a fast-paced environment, and a good sense of humor. Candidates with Pennsylvania ties and customer service experience are strongly encouraged to apply. There is a stipend and transit benefits available for qualifying candidates.
responsibilities: Assist staff with projects in diverse issue areas
qualifications: Preference for those who can stay at least three months
Strong oral and written communication skills
Ability to multitask in a fast-paced environment
how_to_apply: Please e-mail a resume and cover letter including desired start date and availability to lamb.tours@mail.house.gov.
salary_info: Stipend and transit benefits available for qualifying candidates

description:
The DC office of Congressman DesJarlais is looking for summer interns - from June through the end of July.

The responsibilities will vary. Interns will be asked to answer phones, conduct Capitol tours, run errands, research legislation for the Member and legislative staff, attend hearings and briefings and answer constituent letters on various issues before the House. As a result, interns learn about the legislative process and the many other functions of a congressional office.

The hours generally run 9:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m. Monday through Friday.
responsibilities: Research legislation for the Member and legislative staff
Answer constituent letters on various issues before the House
qualifications: Availability from June through the end of July
Ability to work 9:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m. Monday through Friday
how_to_apply: Interested candidates should e-mail an updated resume and cover letter to MeKenna.Carman@mail.house.gov

description:
Congresswoman Lois Frankel's (FL-21) D.C. office seeks interns for the upcoming Summer 2022 semester.

The ideal candidate possesses a high degree of professionalism and maturity, is committed and diligent, and is a team player with a positive attitude. This position demands the ability to multi-task, strong organizational skills and working cooperatively and courteously with others in a shared space.

Duties include: answering telephones, compiling daily press clips relevant to the office's priorities, assisting with constituent correspondence, and providing administrative assistance to the D.C. staff. Undergraduate students and recent college graduates preferred, South Florida ties a plus. Women and minorities are encouraged to apply.
responsibilities: Compiling daily press clips relevant to the office's priorities
Providing administrative assistance to the D.C. staff
qualifications: High degree of professionalism and maturity
Team player with a positive attitude
Ability to work cooperatively and courteously with others in a shared space
Undergraduate students and recent college graduates preferred
how_to_apply: Submit a resume and cover letter in a single PDF file. Cover letter should detail reasons for applying to the position. Please indicate which session is preferred - Session A (2022-05-09 - 2022-06-17) or Session B (2022-06-21 - 2022-07-29). Deadline to apply by is Friday, 2022-04-15. All materials must be submitted to Joelle.Lawrence@mail.house.gov.
equal_opportunity: Women and minorities are encouraged to apply.

description: Congressman Dan Newhouse (WA-04) is currently seeking paid congressional interns for the Summer 2022 term. The internships are in-person and will run from May to August. Dates are flexible. This internship is open to undergraduate and recent college graduates.
responsibilities: Assisting staff with administrative and legislative tasks
qualifications: Undergraduate students or recent college graduates
Ability to work in a fast-paced work environment
District ties considered but not required
how_to_apply: Submit a resume, cover letter, and any writing samples with the subject line "Summer 2022 Internship" to Ben.Savercool@mail.house.gov.

description: The Northwest Florida office is seeking summer interns in Washington, D.C. The summer semester runs from May through August, dates are negotiable. The internship offers opportunities to gain hands-on experience working in a congressional office including legislative research, operations, and communications. Qualified applicants should possess strong writing and communication skills, the ability to multi-task, a positive attitude, a strong sense of professionalism, and a passion for public service. Candidates enthusiastic about the America First movement are encouraged to apply.
qualifications: Enthusiasm about the America First movement encouraged
how_to_apply: Send a resume and cover letter to northwestflorida000@gmail.com with the subject 'Summer Internship Application'. In your cover letter, please indicate expected start and end dates and hours available to work. The application deadline is on a rolling basis.

office: House Committee on Oversight and Reform, Subcommittee on Government Operations (Democratic Staff)
description:
The Democratic staff of the House Committee on Oversight and Reform, Subcommittee on Government Operations, is seeking an energetic and self-motivated graduate or law school student for a full-time internship for Summer 2022.

The position offers the opportunity to gain substantive experience on an exciting and fast-paced Subcommittee, while participating directly in the oversight process and learning about the day-to-day operations of the House of Representatives.

Background: The Committee on Oversight and Reform is the primary investigative committee of the U.S. House of Representatives, with jurisdiction to investigate any matter at any time. The Subcommittee on Government Operations has jurisdiction over issues related to the federal civil service, whistleblower protections, the U.S. Postal Service; government management and accounting measures; the economy, efficiency, and management of government operations and activities; government reorganization; intergovernmental affairs, including state and local governments; federal information technology security, acquisition policy, and management; and federal property.
responsibilities: Assist staff in preparing for congressional hearings
Attend various briefings and meetings with Executive Branch and private sector officials
qualifications: Excellent written and oral communication skills
Willing to be in the office full time
how_to_apply: Interested applicants should apply to this position by emailing their resume and cover letter in one PDF titled 'last name, first name, Spring 2022 GovOps' to GovOpsSubcommittee@gmail.com with 'Spring 2022 Internship Application' in the subject line. No phone calls or drop-ins please. Only applicants who are selected for an interview will receive a response.

description: The Washington, D.C. office of Congresswoman Gwen Moore seeks interns for the upcoming Summer 2022 term. The D.C. office has two types of internships, including a Legislative Internship.
responsibilities: Drafting and editing constituent correspondence letters
Researching legislation for the legislative staff
Providing support for the day-to-day activities of the office
qualifications: Indicate expected start and end dates
how_to_apply: Interested applicants should send a resume, a one-page cover letter, and one short writing sample to WI04DC.Internships@mail.house.gov. Applicants should indicate their expected start and end dates, availability for Monday-Friday, and which position they are applying for. Deadline to apply is 5:00 PM ET on Friday, April 15th, 2022.
equal_opportunity: The office of Congresswoman Moore is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate based on race, ethnicity, gender, sexual orientation, religion, national origin, age, or socio-economic status. All are welcome and encouraged to apply.

description: The Washington, D.C. office of Congresswoman Gwen Moore seeks interns for the upcoming Summer 2022 term. The D.C. office has two types of internships, including a Press Internship.
responsibilities: Conducting interview preparation for the Member
Drafting and editing constituent correspondence letters
Assisting the Communications Director in the day-to-day press operations of the office
qualifications: Indicate expected start and end dates
how_to_apply: Interested applicants should send a resume, a one-page cover letter, and one short writing sample to WI04DC.Internships@mail.house.gov. Applicants should indicate their expected start and end dates, availability for Monday-Friday, and which position they are applying for. Applicants for the Press Internship must submit 1-3 sample graphics. Deadline to apply is 5:00 PM ET on Friday, April 15th, 2022.
equal_opportunity: The office of Congresswoman Moore is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate based on race, ethnicity, gender, sexual orientation, religion, national origin, age, or socio-economic status. All are welcome and encouraged to apply.

description:
Congresswoman Maria Elvira Salazar (FL-27) is currently accepting applicants for interns in her Miami and Washington, D.C. offices for the summer session.

Summer interns will begin their internship programs no earlier than May 1 and conclude their program no later than August 15. Interns are expected to serve in the office at a minimum of 15 hours each week during their tenure.
responsibilities: Attend community events and Congressional briefings
Perform data entry, including logging letters and constituent requests
Log constituent correspondence, including names, addresses, and relevant information
Perform other duties as assigned by the intern coordinator
qualifications: Good oral and written communication skills
Ability to work cooperatively and courteously with others
Ties to South Florida and means of travel are a plus
how_to_apply: The deadline for applications for the Summer 2022 internship session is April 30. Interested applicants may submit their resume and a cover letter to Howard Senior at hs@mail.house.gov (Miami) or Liam Diebel at liam.diebel@mail.house.gov (Washington, D.C.), in addition to completing the online application available at https://salazar.house.gov/services/internships.

description:
The Lacey Office of Congresswoman Marilyn Strickland (WA-10) is currently accepting applications for a paid summer internship (June-August).

Interns will be asked to answer phones, assist the constituent services team, attend virtual events, research projects, and aid our press team, Congresswoman, and district staff. As a result, interns learn about constituent services and engagement and the many other functions of a congressional office.
responsibilities: Aid press team, Congresswoman, and district staff
qualifications: Ability to work in a fast-paced environment
how_to_apply: Submit a resume, cover letter, a one-page writing sample, and dates of availability in a PDF format to WA10resumes@gmail.com with "District Office Intern-[LAST NAME]" in the subject line. The deadline for applications is Friday, April 15, 2022. Applicants are encouraged to submit their application early.
equal_opportunity: This office is an Equal Employment Opportunity Office and does not discriminate in employment based on race, color, religion, sex, gender identity, national origin, sexual orientation, marital status, disability, genetic information, age, military service, or another non-merit factor. BIPOC applicants are encouraged to apply.

description: The office of Congresswoman Marilyn Strickland (WA-10) is currently accepting applicants for paid summer internships which will run from June to August. Legislative Interns assist the legislative staff located in Washington D.C. in conducting research, drafting memos and constituent mail, answering phones, attending hearings, inputting casework, attending virtual events, aiding Congresswoman Strickland's legislative initiatives. The office is also looking to hire a press intern to assist the press team. Ideal candidates for these internships should possess excellent communication skills, strong attention to detail, the ability to work in a fast-paced environment, and a good sense of humor. The deadline for applications is Friday, April 15, 2022, but applicants are encouraged to submit their application early. Applications that are incomplete or submitted after the deadline will not be considered.
responsibilities: Draft social media posts, op-eds, press releases, and digital content
Work with the press team to devise media strategies
qualifications: Ability to work in a fast-paced environment
Experience working in press, communications, or a related field is a plus
how_to_apply: Submit a resume, cover letter, a one-page writing sample, and dates of availability in a single PDF document to WA10resumes@gmail.com with "DC Summer Internship-[LAST NAME]" in the subject line. Please indicate in the cover letter which internship you are applying for. Applications that are incomplete or submitted after the deadline will not be considered.
equal_opportunity: This office is Equal Employment Opportunity Office and does not discriminate in employment based on race, color, religion, sex, gender identity, national origin, sexual orientation, marital status, disability, genetic information, age, military service, or another non-merit factor. BIPOC applicants and individuals in underrepresented communities are encouraged to apply.

office: House Committee on Oversight and Reform Majority Staff
description: The House Committee on Oversight and Reform Majority Staff seeks a motivated and reliable undergraduate or graduate student to fill a full-time digital intern position this summer. This position reports to the communications director.
responsibilities: Performing other press and administrative tasks as necessary
qualifications: Familiarity with Adobe Premiere Pro, Adobe Photoshop, Adobe InDesign, and Canva
Ability to compile and interpret social media analytics
Ability to work in a fast-paced, rapid response environment
how_to_apply: To apply, please send a resume and portfolio to oversightpress@mail.house.gov.
equal_opportunity: Candidates of all backgrounds are encouraged to apply.

office: House Committee on Oversight and Reform Majority Staff
description: The House Committee on Oversight and Reform Majority Staff seeks a motivated, reliable, and detail-oriented undergraduate or graduate student with a passion for communications to fill a full-time press intern position this summer. The ideal candidate should be a strong writer, team player, voracious consumer of news, and able to multi-task in a fast-paced environment. Press intern responsibilities include, but are not limited to, compiling press clips, maintaining press lists, and drafting press releases. This position reports to the communications director.
qualifications: Able to multi-task in a fast-paced environment
how_to_apply: To apply, please send a resume and cover letter or writing sample to oversightpress@mail.house.gov.
equal_opportunity: Candidates of all backgrounds are encouraged to apply.

office: House Committee on Education and Labor (Democratic Staff)
description: The House Committee on Education and Labor (Democratic staff) seeks a full-time Press Intern for paid and unpaid internships. Duties may include, but are not limited to, media monitoring and research, writing media advisories and press releases, drafting social media and general communications office support. Ideal candidate possesses strong writing skills, works well under pressure, and is a skilled multi-tasker. Interns can receive academic credit.
responsibilities: Writing media advisories and press releases
Drafting social media and general communications
qualifications: Ability to work well under pressure
how_to_apply: Applications will be considered on a rolling basis until June 1, 2022 for the summer and fall semesters. To apply, please email a cover letter, resume, three references, and two writing samples to E&L.Jobs@mail.house.gov with "Press Intern" in the subject line. Applications missing any of the requirements will not be considered.
equal_opportunity: The office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, gender identity, or sexual orientation. Candidates of diverse backgrounds are strongly encouraged to apply.

description: The Washington, D.C. Office of Congressman Josh Gottheimer (NJ-05) is seeking motivated, organized, and hardworking individuals for the summer term. Successful candidates will possess excellent written and oral communication, willingness to contribute to a team environment, and eagerness to learn about the political process and congressional affairs. New Jersey ties are a plus.
qualifications: Excellent written and oral communication skills
Willingness to contribute to a team environment
Eagerness to learn about the political process and congressional affairs
how_to_apply: Please send a resume, cover letter, one-page writing sample, and dates of availability as one PDF with subject line "DC SUMMER INTERNSHIP 2022" to nj5jobs@mail.house.gov.
